title: Mock Test 05 — Underground Coal & Metal Mining

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The perimeter of a square of side $9$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perimeter of a square $=4\times\text{side}$.

**Working.** $4\times 9 = 36$.

**Answer.** $36$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the area of the triangle shown with base $6$ and height $4$.

figure.caption: Triangle base and height
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,40' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='120' x2='100' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='90' y='135' font-size='8'>base 6</text><text x='105' y='85' font-size='8'>height 4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\tfrac{1}{2}bh$.

**Working.**
$$A=\tfrac{1}{2}(6)(4)=12$$

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The simple interest on $5000$ at $8\%$ per annum for $3$ years is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{SI}=\dfrac{P\,r\,t}{100}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{5000\times 8\times 3}{100}=1200$$

**Answer.** $1200$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the right triangle shown, the legs are $3$ and $4$. The length of the hypotenuse is _____ .

figure.caption: Right triangle with legs 3 and 4 and the right angle at the bottom-left.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='40,130 160,130 40,40' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='118' width='12' height='12' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1'/><text x='95' y='145' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>4</text><text x='20' y='90' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>3</text><text x='105' y='80' font-size='12' fill='#ef4444'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pythagoras: hypotenuse $=\sqrt{a^2+b^2}$.

**Working.** $\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=\sqrt{25}=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article marked $25\%$ above its cost price is sold at a $10\%$ discount. The profit percentage is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply markup then discount on a base cost of $100$.

**Working.** CP $=100$, MP $=125$, SP $=125\times0.9=112.5$; profit $=12.5\%$.

**Answer.** $12.5\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a row, C is $9$th from the left and $11$th from the right. How many people are in the row?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total $=$ left rank $+$ right rank $-1$ (C counted once).

**Working.** $9+11-1=19$.

**Answer.** $19$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Arrange the sentences into a coherent paragraph.
P: As a result, the roof was reinforced with additional bolts.
Q: During inspection, engineers noticed hairline cracks in the roof.
R: They immediately evacuated the section as a precaution.
S: A detailed survey then confirmed that the rock was unstable.
The correct order is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Arrange by cause-and-effect and chronological cues.

**Working.** Cracks are noticed (Q), the area is evacuated as a precaution (R), a survey confirms instability (S), and as a result bolts are added (P).

**Answer.** Q, R, S, P.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sum of money doubles itself in $8$ years under simple interest. The rate of interest per annum is _____ % .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Doubling means the interest equals the principal.

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{P\,r\,8}{100}\Rightarrow r=\dfrac{100}{8}=12.5$.

**Answer.** $12.5\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which word reads the same when viewed in a vertical mirror?

figure.caption: The word MOM with a dashed vertical mirror line to its right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='30' y='72' font-size='52' fill='#334155'>MOM</text><line x1='195' y1='12' x2='195' y2='98' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each letter must be vertically symmetric and the word a palindrome.

**Working.** M, O, M are all vertically symmetric and 'MOM' is a palindrome, so the mirror image is identical.

**Answer.** MOM.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: The numbers in the firing pattern shown are millisecond delays between holes. Such delay initiation is used mainly to:
options: improve fragmentation and reduce ground vibration by sequencing the holes
make all holes detonate at exactly the same instant

figure.caption: Delay sequence between holes
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#475569'><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='4'/><circle cx='90' cy='60' r='4'/><circle cx='140' cy='60' r='4'/><circle cx='190' cy='60' r='4'/></g><text x='32' y='85' font-size='8'>0</text><text x='80' y='85' font-size='8'>25</text><text x='126' y='85' font-size='8'>50</text><text x='176' y='85' font-size='8'>75 ms</text><text x='40' y='40' font-size='8'>delay sequence</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Delay blasting controls the timing of individual holes.

**Reasoning.** Firing holes in sequence provides relief for later holes, improving fragmentation and muckpile shape while spreading the charge weight per delay, which lowers peak ground vibration.

**Answer.** Improve fragmentation and reduce ground vibration by sequencing the holes.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: For the same offsets (0, 2, 4, 3, 0 m at 10 m interval), Simpson's rule gives an area, in m², of ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $A=\dfrac{d}{3}\left[o_1+o_n+4\sum o_{odd}+2\sum o_{even}\right]$.

**Working.**
$$A=\frac{10}{3}\big[(0+0)+4(2+3)+2(4)\big]=\frac{10}{3}(28)=93.33\ \text{m}^2$$

**Answer.** 93.33 m².

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A square pillar has a $10\ \text{m}\times10\ \text{m}$ plan and is $8\ \text{m}$ high (figure). Compute its volume ($\mathrm{m^3}$).

figure.caption: Square pillar
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='60,60 120,60 150,40 90,40' fill='#9ca3af' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='60' height='70' fill='#78716c' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='120,60 150,40 150,110 120,130' fill='#6b7280' stroke='#475569'/><text x='26' y='160' font-size='8'>plan 10x10, height 8</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $V=\text{base area}\times\text{height}$.

**Working.**
$$V=10\times10\times8=800\ \mathrm{m^3}$$

**Answer.** $800\ \mathrm{m^3}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The layout shown — coal extracted in galleries leaving regular square pillars to support the roof — is which method?

figure.caption: Method plan
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='180' fill='#e5e7eb'/><g fill='#1f2937'><rect x='30' y='30' width='45' height='45'/><rect x='115' y='30' width='45' height='45'/><rect x='30' y='115' width='45' height='45'/><rect x='115' y='115' width='45' height='45'/></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Regular pillars left between driven galleries characterise bord-and-pillar mining.

**Reasoning.** The grid of square coal pillars supporting the roof while galleries are driven around them is the defining geometry of the bord-and-pillar (room-and-pillar) method.

**Answer.** Bord-and-pillar (room-and-pillar).

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: From the bar chart of present values shown (PV of benefits ₹150, PV of costs ₹100), compute the Benefit–Cost Ratio.

figure.caption: Benefit vs cost
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='190' y2='130' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='50' y='40' width='40' height='90' fill='#16a34a'/><rect x='120' y='70' width='40' height='60' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='48' y='32' font-size='8'>PV B=150</text><text x='116' y='62' font-size='8'>PV C=100</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** BCR is the ratio of present worth of benefits to present worth of costs.

**Working.**
$$BCR = \frac{150}{100} = 1.5$$

**Answer.** BCR $= \mathbf{1.5}$ (project is economically attractive, BCR > 1).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Effective Rate
questions.stem: A nominal interest rate of $12\%$ per year is compounded monthly. The effective annual rate is _____ percent. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: For a layout of 15 m square pillars separated by 5 m galleries (as shown), compute the extraction ratio.

figure.caption: Pillar plan
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='180' fill='#e5e7eb'/><g fill='#1f2937'><rect x='35' y='35' width='45' height='45'/><rect x='115' y='35' width='45' height='45'/><rect x='35' y='115' width='45' height='45'/><rect x='115' y='115' width='45' height='45'/></g><text x='88' y='62' font-size='9' fill='#475569'>B=5</text><text x='44' y='62' font-size='9' fill='#fff'>W=15</text></svg>

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Using the trapezoidal rule with the three ordinates $f_0=1$, $f_1=2$, $f_2=5$ at spacing $h=1$, estimate the integral.

figure.caption: Ordinates for numerical integration
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='200' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='40' y1='120' x2='40' y2='110' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='110' y1='120' x2='110' y2='90' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='180' y1='120' x2='180' y2='40' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='35' y='105' font-size='8'>1</text><text x='105' y='85' font-size='8'>2</text><text x='175' y='35' font-size='8'>5</text><text x='40' y='140' font-size='8'>ordinates, h=1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trapezoidal rule: $I=\dfrac{h}{2}(f_0+2f_1+f_2)$.

**Working.**
$$I=\frac{1}{2}(1+2(2)+5)=\frac{1}{2}(10)=5$$

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: For a critical-width panel, the subsidence factor is $a=0.65$ and the extracted seam thickness is $m=3\ \text{m}$. Compute the maximum subsidence (m).

figure.caption: Subsidence trough over a panel
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='210' y2='40' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M40 40 Q110 95 180 40' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='95' x2='160' y2='95' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='3'/><text x='25' y='112' font-size='8'>extracted panel, a=0.65, m=3 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $S_{max}=a\times m$.

**Working.**
$$S_{max}=0.65\times3=1.95\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $1.95$ m.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Future Worth
questions.stem: $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}000$ is deposited at $5\%$ for one year (annual compounding). The amount at year-end is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Griffith's theory of brittle failure attributes fracture to
options: stress concentration at the tips of pre-existing microcracks
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Griffith flaws act as stress raisers.

**Working.** Tensile stress concentrates at crack tips and drives propagation.

**Answer.** stress concentration at the tips of pre-existing microcracks.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: For $t_o=4$ and $t_p=8$ days, the PERT variance of the activity is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma^2=\left(\dfrac{t_p-t_o}{6}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma^2=\left(\frac{8-4}{6}\right)^2=\left(\frac{4}{6}\right)^2=0.44.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 0.44$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: For the same curve (R = 200 m, Δ = 60°), the length of the curve, in m, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=R\,\Delta$ with Δ in radians.

**Working.**
$$L=200\times\frac{60\pi}{180}=200\times1.0472=209.44\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 209.44 m.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Fixed cost is Rs $300000$, selling price is Rs $50$ per tonne and variable cost is Rs $30$ per tonne (figure). Compute the break-even quantity (tonnes).

figure.caption: Break-even chart
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='30' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='205' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='100' x2='200' y2='40' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='80' x2='200' y2='80' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><circle cx='128' cy='65' r='3' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='35' y='28' font-size='8'>revenue vs cost</text><text x='96' y='138' font-size='8'>BEQ?</text></svg>

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Line drilling as a controlled-blasting technique uses
options: a row of closely spaced unloaded (empty) holes that form a plane of weakness guiding the break
detonating cord laid on the surface
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Line drilling provides a guide plane without explosive.

**Reasoning.** Many closely spaced empty holes create a weakened surface along the final line; the production blast then tends to break to this plane, limiting overbreak. It is often combined with presplit/smooth blasting.

**Answer.** Closely spaced empty holes forming a weakness plane.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Two vertical boreholes $40\ \text{m}$ apart (along the dip direction) intersect the same seam, with the seam $40\ \text{m}$ deeper in the down-dip hole. Compute the apparent dip along that line (degrees).

figure.caption: Dip from two boreholes
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='50' y1='20' x2='50' y2='90' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='150' y1='20' x2='150' y2='130' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='90' x2='150' y2='130' stroke='#b45309' stroke-width='3'/><text x='28' y='150' font-size='8'>holes 40 m apart, seam 40 m deeper</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{dip}=\arctan\left(\dfrac{\Delta\text{depth}}{\text{spacing}}\right)$.

**Working.**
$$\text{dip}=\arctan\left(\frac{40}{40}\right)=\arctan(1)=45^\circ$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $500\,\text{Pa}$ at $600\,\text{rpm}$. At $720\,\text{rpm}$ it develops _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P\propto N^2$.

**Working.**
$$P_2=500\times\left(\frac{720}{600}\right)^2=500\times1.44=720.$$

**Answer.** $720\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Metamorphic rocks are produced when pre-existing rocks are altered by
options: heat, pressure and chemically active fluids while essentially remaining solid
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metamorphism is solid-state change.

**Reasoning.** Elevated temperature and/or pressure (and fluids) recrystallise minerals and develop new textures without melting; e.g. limestone → marble, shale → slate, granite → gneiss.

**Answer.** Heat, pressure and fluids (solid state).

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The main benefit of in-pit crushing and conveying (IPCC) over an all-truck system is
options: reduced truck haulage distance/cost and lower diesel and tyre consumption
questions.solution:
**Concept.** IPCC brings the crusher to the pit and conveys crushed rock out.

**Reasoning.** Trucks need only make a short haul to the in-pit crusher; the belt does the long, deep lift. This cuts diesel, tyre and maintenance costs and truck fleet size.

**Answer.** Reduced truck haulage distance and cost.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Slope stability radar (SSR) on a pit wall is used to
options: measure ore grade in the wall
monitor real-time wall movement and give early warning of impending failure
survey the pit topography for volume
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Wall failures are preceded by accelerating displacement.

**Reasoning.** Ground-based radar scans the wall continuously and detects sub-millimetre movement over large areas, allowing alarms to be raised and the area evacuated before collapse.

**Answer.** Real-time movement monitoring / early warning.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A dozer moves 3 m³ (loose) of material per 0.5-min push-return cycle. Its loose production, in m³/h, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Production $=\dfrac{\text{load per cycle}}{\text{cycle time}}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\frac{3}{0.5}=6\ \text{m}^3/\text{min}=6\times60=360\ \text{m}^3/\text{h}$$

**Answer.** 360 m³/h (loose).

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A skip of dead weight 5 t carries a 10 t payload. Ignoring rope weight and acceleration, the static rope tension, in kN, is ____ (two decimals). Take g = 9.81 m/s².
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Static tension $=$ (dead + payload) $\times g$.

**Working.**
$$T=(5000+10000)\times9.81=147{,}150\ \text{N}=147.15\ \text{kN}$$

**Answer.** 147.15 kN.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Total operating cost for a period is Rs $600000$ for an output of $30000\ \text{t}$ (figure). Compute the unit cost (Rs/t).

figure.caption: Unit cost
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='40' width='120' height='60' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='55' y='75' font-size='9'>total cost 600000</text><text x='44' y='125' font-size='8'>output 30000 t -> unit cost?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Unit cost $=\dfrac{\text{total cost}}{\text{output}}$.

**Working.**
$$c=\frac{600000}{30000}=20\ \text{Rs/t}$$

**Answer.** Rs $20$ per tonne.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A shuttle car in a bord-and-pillar coal mine
options: is a rubber-tyred car that carries coal from the continuous miner to a feeder/conveyor
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shuttle cars bridge the face to the main transport.

**Reasoning.** They shuttle back and forth between the continuous miner and the section feeder-breaker/belt, providing flexible, trackless face haulage in room-and-pillar mining.

**Answer.** Carries coal from continuous miner to feeder/conveyor.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The force required to hold a $200\,\text{N}$ block in equilibrium on a smooth incline of $30^\circ$, applied parallel to the incline, is _____ N.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On a smooth incline the holding force equals $W\sin\theta$.

**Working.**
$$F=200\sin 30^\circ=100.$$

**Answer.** $100\,\text{N}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The two stages of bord-and-pillar (room-and-pillar) coal mining are
options: development (driving bords, leaving pillars) and depillaring (extracting the pillars)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bord-and-pillar works in two passes.

**Reasoning.** First, galleries (bords) are driven on a grid leaving coal pillars that support the roof (development/first working); later the pillars themselves are extracted on retreat (depillaring/second working).

**Answer.** Development and depillaring.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a rock with $E=60\,\text{GPa}$ and $\nu=0.25$, the shear modulus $G=\dfrac{E}{2(1+\nu)}$ is _____ GPa.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A stope yields $18\ \text{t}$ of ore together with $2\ \text{t}$ of wall waste (figure). Compute the dilution (%).

figure.caption: Ore with wall dilution
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='50' y='40' width='100' height='60' fill='#a16207' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='50' y='40' width='100' height='10' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='62' y='78' font-size='8' fill='#ffffff'>ore 18</text><text x='58' y='128' font-size='8'>wall dilution 2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$D=\frac{2}{18+2}\times100=\frac{2}{20}\times100=10\%$$

**Answer.** $10\%$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In the plan shown, square pillars (w = 15 m) are separated by 5 m galleries (B). The tributary area assigned to each pillar, in m², is ____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 360 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='0' y='0' width='360' height='220' fill='#ffffff'/><g fill='#9ca3af' stroke='#1f2937'><rect x='40' y='30' width='60' height='60'/><rect x='120' y='30' width='60' height='60'/><rect x='200' y='30' width='60' height='60'/><rect x='40' y='110' width='60' height='60'/><rect x='120' y='110' width='60' height='60'/><rect x='200' y='110' width='60' height='60'/></g><rect x='110' y='20' width='80' height='80' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5 4'/><text x='66' y='64' font-size='12' fill='#111111'>w</text><text x='104' y='106' font-size='10' fill='#111111'>B</text><text x='96' y='205' font-size='11' fill='#dc2626'>tributary cell = (w+B)^2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Plan of square pillars on regular centres; the dashed cell (pillar + surrounding half-galleries) is the tributary area.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tributary area $=(w+B)^2$.

**Working.**
$$A=(15+5)^2=20^2=400\ \text{m}^2$$

**Answer.** 400 m².

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: For a project with positive future cash flows, increasing the discount rate will
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Higher discount rates shrink the present worth of future flows.

**Working.** Hence NPV falls.

**Answer.** lower its net present value.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A dip-slip fault has a net slip of 10 m along a fault plane dipping 30°. The throw (vertical component), in m, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Throw $=$ net slip $\times\sin(\text{dip})$.

**Working.**
$$\text{Throw}=10\sin30^{\circ}=10\times0.5=5.0\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 5.0 m.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Loading time per truck is 2 min and the truck cycle time is 12 min. The number of trucks giving a match factor of exactly 1.0 is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $MF=\dfrac{N\times t_{load}}{t_{cycle}}$; set $MF=1$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{t_{cycle}}{t_{load}}=\frac{12}{2}=6$$

**Answer.** 6 trucks.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A wheeled scraper is most economical for
options: loading, hauling and spreading loose material over short to medium distances
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A scraper loads, transports and discharges material in a single unit.

**Reasoning.** It is efficient in soft, free-digging ground over short-to-medium hauls, e.g. topsoil and soft overburden removal; it cannot handle hard rock.

**Answer.** Loading, hauling and spreading loose material over short/medium distances.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A skip (5 t) carrying 10 t payload hangs on a 2600 kg rope. The total suspended weight, in kN, is ____ (two decimals). Take g = 9.81 m/s².
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Weight $=$ (skip + payload + rope mass) $\times g$.

**Working.**
$$m=5000+10000+2600=17{,}600\ \text{kg}$$
$$W=17{,}600\times9.81=172{,}656\ \text{N}=172.66\ \text{kN}$$

**Answer.** 172.66 kN.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a rock with $\phi=40^\circ$, the Mohr-Coulomb failure plane makes an angle of _____ degrees with the major principal plane direction ($45^\circ+\phi/2$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\theta=45^\circ+\phi/2$.

**Working.**
$$45^\circ+20^\circ=65^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $65^\circ$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: If the powder factor is 0.48 kg/m³ and the rock density is 2.5 t/m³, the powder factor, in kg/tonne, is ____ (three decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $PF_{mass}=\dfrac{PF_{vol}}{\rho}$.

**Working.**
$$PF=\frac{0.48}{2.5}=0.192\ \text{kg/t}$$

**Answer.** 0.192 kg/tonne.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: In CPM network analysis, the critical path is
options: the longest path through the network, having zero total float
the shortest path through the network
the path with the most activities
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The critical path governs project duration.

**Working.** It is the longest path; its activities have zero total float.

**Answer.** the longest path with zero total float.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The overall stripping ratio of an opencast mine is
options: the waste-to-ore ratio for the next incremental push only
the total waste divided by the total ore over the mine life
the bench face angle divided by the overall slope angle
the ratio of loose to bank volume
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Several stripping ratios are defined.

**Reasoning.** The overall (average) SR is the ratio of all waste to be moved to all ore to be recovered over the entire life of the pit; it sizes the total waste task.

**Answer.** Total waste / total ore over mine life.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The principal purpose of a barrier pillar is to
options: isolate panels/old workings (against water, gas and stress) and protect main roadways
support the headframe at the surface
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Barrier pillars are large protective separators.

**Reasoning.** Left between panels or around mains, they prevent inrush of water/gas from old workings, shield main roadways from panel abutment stress, and limit propagation of failures.

**Answer.** Isolate workings and protect mains.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: To keep vibration within limits a minimum scaled distance of 25 must be maintained. For a structure 50 m away (as shown), compute the maximum allowable charge per delay (kg).

figure.caption: Charge limit by distance
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='90' r='10' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='22' y='115' font-size='8'>blast</text><rect x='190' y='70' width='30' height='40' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><text x='176' y='125' font-size='8'>structure</text><line x1='52' y1='90' x2='188' y2='90' stroke='#475569' stroke-dasharray='4 3' marker-end='url(#sd)'/><defs><marker id='sd' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='100' y='84' font-size='8'>R=50 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** From $SD = R/\sqrt{W}$, the maximum charge is $W = (R/SD)^2$.

**Working.**
$$W = \left(\frac{50}{25}\right)^2 = 2^2 = 4$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{4}$ kg per delay.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: With 4.5 m³ loose per pass and 120 cycles per hour, the loose output, in m³/h, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Loose hourly output $=$ load per pass $\times$ cycles per hour.

**Working.**
$$Q_L=4.5\times120=540\ \text{m}^3/\text{h}$$

**Answer.** 540 m³/h (loose).

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Cross-sections at the two ends (30 m apart) have areas 20 m² and 40 m², and the mid-section area is 32 m². By the prismoidal formula the volume, in m³, is ____.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bucket wheel excavator (BWE) is best suited to
options: continuous excavation of soft, unconsolidated overburden
selective mining of narrow high-grade veins
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The BWE is a continuous, high-capacity digging machine with limited cutting force.

**Reasoning.** It excels in soft, free-digging overburden (lignite mines) where no drilling/blasting is needed, feeding a continuous conveyor; it cannot dig hard rock.

**Answer.** Continuous excavation of soft, unconsolidated overburden.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A firedamp explosion is especially dangerous in a dusty coal mine because it can
options: raise and ignite deposited coal dust, triggering a far more violent and far-reaching secondary coal-dust explosion
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A small gas ignition can escalate via dust.

**Reasoning.** The blast and flame of even a minor firedamp ignition can entrain settled coal dust and set off a coal-dust explosion that propagates throughout the workings — historically the cause of the worst mine disasters.

**Answer.** Trigger a secondary coal-dust explosion.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A continuous surface mining system (excavator–conveyor–spreader) differs from a shovel–truck system mainly in that
options: it handles material as a continuous flow rather than discrete truck loads
it requires drilling and blasting of every bench
it works only in hard, abrasive rock
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continuous systems link digging, transport and dumping in an uninterrupted chain.

**Reasoning.** A bucket-wheel excavator feeds a belt conveyor that feeds a spreader, moving material as a steady stream — eliminating the cyclic load/haul/dump pattern of shovel-truck operation. It suits soft, free-digging ground.

**Answer.** Continuous material flow rather than discrete loads.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: A closed traverse has a misclosure in easting of $0.3\ \text{m}$ and in northing of $0.4\ \text{m}$ (figure). Compute the linear closing error (m).

figure.caption: Traverse misclosure
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,130 160,130 130,40 50,60' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='52' y2='118' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='3'/><text x='28' y='150' font-size='8'>dE=0.3, dN=0.4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Closing error $=\sqrt{\Delta E^2+\Delta N^2}$.

**Working.**
$$e=\sqrt{0.3^2+0.4^2}=\sqrt{0.25}=0.5\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $0.5$ m.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The general solution of $y''-4y'+4y=0$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Repeated root gives a solution with a factor of $x$.

**Working.**
$$r^{2}-4r+4=(r-2)^{2}=0\Rightarrow r=2\text{ (double)}$$
Solution $(C_1+C_2 x)e^{2x}$.

**Answer.** $(C_1+C_2 x)e^{2x}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rockburst is best described as
options: a sudden, violent failure releasing stored elastic strain energy
questions.solution:
**Concept.** High stored strain energy in brittle, highly stressed rock.

**Working.** Its sudden release ejects rock violently.

**Answer.** a sudden, violent failure releasing stored elastic strain energy.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For principal stresses $\sigma_1=80$ and $\sigma_2=20\,\text{MPa}$, the shear stress on a plane at $45^\circ$ is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** At $45^\circ$, $\tau=\dfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_2}{2}$.

**Working.**
$$\tau=\frac{80-20}{2}=30.$$

**Answer.** $30\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A flame safety lamp is used underground to
options: detect methane (by the gas cap on the flame) and indicate oxygen deficiency
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The flame responds to gas and to oxygen content.

**Reasoning.** Methane forms a pale-blue 'cap' above the flame whose height indicates its percentage, while a falling flame warns of oxygen deficiency; the gauze prevents the flame igniting the surrounding atmosphere.

**Answer.** Detect methane and oxygen deficiency.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: In engineering economy, MARR stands for
questions.solution:
**Concept.** MARR is the hurdle rate for accepting projects.

**Working.** It is the Minimum Attractive Rate of Return.

**Answer.** Minimum Attractive Rate of Return.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The roots of the characteristic equation of $y''+2y'+5y=0$ are
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Use $r=\dfrac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^{2}-4ac}}{2a}$.

**Working.**
$$r=\frac{-2\pm\sqrt{4-20}}{2}=\frac{-2\pm 4i}{2}=-1\pm 2i$$

**Answer.** $-1\pm 2i$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: With sub-drill = 0.3 × burden, the sub-drilling depth for a 4.5 m burden, in m, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $J=0.3\,B$.

**Working.**
$$J=0.3\times4.5=1.35\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 1.35 m.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The ultimate (economic) pit limit is reached where
options: the incremental stripping cost equals the incremental ore value
all the ore is exhausted regardless of cost
the bench height equals the burden
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond this depth, mining the next ore costs more than it returns.

**Working.** This is the break-even stripping-ratio boundary.

**Answer.** the incremental stripping cost equals the incremental ore value.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In the Brazilian test shown, a disc of diameter $D=50\ \mathrm{mm}$ and thickness $L=50\ \mathrm{mm}$ fails at a load $P=30\ \mathrm{kN}$. Using $\sigma_t=\dfrac{2P}{\pi D L}$, the tensile strength, in MPa, is _____ (two decimals).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='90' cy='95' r='45' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='40' y1='45' x2='140' y2='45' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='40' y1='145' x2='140' y2='145' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='90' y1='25' x2='90' y2='42' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#bz)'/><defs><marker id='bz' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='96' y='20' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>P = 30 kN</text><text x='52' y='170' font-size='9'>D=50 mm, L=50 mm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Brazilian disc under diametral load

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_t=\dfrac{2P}{\pi D L}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_t=\frac{2\times30000}{\pi\times0.05\times0.05}=\frac{60000}{0.007854}=7.64\times10^{6}\ \text{Pa}=7.64\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $7.64$ MPa.
